rationale: The citing paper uses Kumar, Sunder, and Leone (2014) in its core framing of sales training effectiveness and repeatedly treats it as a key prior study in Table 1, including its setting, method, and main findings. The 2022 paper explicitly contrasts its own context with that prior work (traditional training versus voluntary online modular training) to motivate its research questions. It also positions added analyses (heterogeneity and spillovers) as contributions beyond that baseline, so the citation plays a meaningful positioning role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Kumar et al. (2014) by moving the training-performance inquiry to a voluntary online modular program and adding spillover and dynamic impact analyses on sales outcomes. The cited paper is used as a benchmark prior study that the new work builds beyond in context and scope.
